long_stay: University accredited study programmes (Bachelor's, Master's, Doctoral)
Language and professional preparation for university accredited programmes (organised by a public university, as part of an EU programme, or based on an international agreement)
Secondary school or higher vocational studies as part of an exchange programme
Exchange of experience studies as part of EU programmes or international agreements
qualifying_courses.notes: Non-accredited courses or private language school courses usually require a 'Visa for Other Purposes'.

types.name: Long-term Visa for Study Purpose
types.notes: Primarily for shorter study stays of several months, up to 1 year.

types.name: Long-term Residence Permit for Study Purpose
types.notes: Primarily for stays of over one year, such as full university programmes.

booking_methods.description: Appointments are scheduled by email, phone, or online booking system, depending on the specific diplomatic mission.

steps.title: Get accepted to a qualifying Czech educational institution
Fill in the official application form
steps.description: Fill online, print, and sign, or download and fill by hand legibly in block capitals and Latin characters.

steps.title: Book an appointment
steps.description: Schedule at your nearest Czech embassy or consulate.

steps.title: Attend in person, submit documents, pay fee, and provide fingerprints
steps.description: An interview will be held. Children younger than 12 do not submit fingerprints and do not need to visit the consulate.

steps.title: Present proof of travel medical insurance and payment of premium
steps.description: Required upon request before the embassy affixes the visa sticker to your travel document.

required_documents.name: Completed Official Application Form
required_documents.details: Filled out and signed, in Czech or officially translated. Can be filled online and printed, or by hand in block capitals and Latin characters.
required_documents.notes: Documents must be no older than 180 days.

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: Valid for at least 3 months beyond the intended stay.

required_documents.name: Letter of Acceptance / Proof of Enrolment
required_documents.details: Original document from the Czech educational institution.

required_documents.name: Proof of Accommodation
required_documents.details: A lease agreement, dormitory reservation, or a 'Proof of Accommodation' form signed by the landlord with a notarized signature (legalized by a Czech notary or Czech Point).

required_documents.name: Criminal Record Background Check
required_documents.details: From your home country and any country where you lived for 6+ months in the last 3 years. Requires an Apostille or super-legalization. Must be translated into Czech by a certified translator with an official rubber stamp.

required_documents.name: Travel Medical Insurance
required_documents.details: Comprehensive insurance required, often from Pojišťovna VZP (PVZP) since 2021. Must be valid from the day of entry. Submitted after visa approval, not at the time of application.

acceptable_proof: Bank statement in your name with an internationally recognized debit card

residence_permit.notes: Upon arrival, registration is required: within 3 working days at the Foreign Police for long-term visa holders, or within 30 calendar days at a Ministry of the Interior office for D/VR visa holders (for collection of a residence permit). After registration, an appointment for biometric data (fingerprints/photo) will be scheduled, followed by a separate appointment to collect the biometric residence card within 60 days from biometric data scanning.

restrictions: Study must remain the primary purpose of the stay.
work_while_studying.notes: Non-EU students enrolled in an accredited degree program have free access to the labor market and do not need a work permit. While there is no legal limit on working hours, students typically work part-time (up to 20 hours per week) to balance academics.

permit_renewal.notes: Long-term residence permits for university accredited programmes can be extended repeatedly for a maximum of 2 years each time. For language or professional preparation organised by a public university, the long-term residence permit can be extended only once.

requirements: Completion of a university-accredited study programme in the Czech Republic
post_study_work.notes: The 9-month job-seeking residence permit grants free access to the labour market and is not extendable. It also makes graduates eligible to apply for a long-term residence permit for doing business without needing 5 years of prior residence. The Employee Card, which acts as a combined work/residence permit, is valid for a maximum of 2 years and is renewable. For Czech graduates, the labor market test for the Employee Card is waived.

official_sources.name: Ministry of the Interior – Foreigners Portal

application_forms.name: Application for a long-term visa for a stay over 90 days
Application for a long-term residence permit
Application for issuing or extending an employee card

tips.title: Legalization of Documents
tips.body: Ensure that documents like the Criminal Record check and Proof of Funds have an Apostille or 'Super-legalization' to be valid in the Czech Republic.

tips.title: Official Translations
tips.body: All documents (except passport) must be translated into Czech by a certified Czech court translator. Translations from foreign agencies are often rejected.

tips.title: Notarized Accommodation Signature
tips.body: The landlord's signature on the proof of accommodation must be notarized (legalized) by a Czech notary or at a Czech Point.

tips.title: Apply Early
tips.body: The legal processing time is 60 days, but it can extend to 90 days or more, especially during peak seasons (July/August). Apply as early as possible, ideally 3-4 months before your intended study start.

tips.title: Monitor Communication
tips.body: Regularly check your email/mail for 'Request to Supplement' from the Ministry. Failure to respond in time can lead to application rejection.

tips.title: Register Upon Arrival
tips.body: Register with the Foreign Police within 3 working days of entry (for long-term visa holders) or with the Ministry of Interior within 30 days (for D/VR visa holders), unless your accommodation provider does it for you.

tips.title: Maintain Valid Health Insurance
tips.body: Ensure your travel medical insurance is valid from the day of entry. Without it, your visa sticker cannot be affixed even after approval.

tips.title: Choose Correct Visa Type
tips.body: If your studies last more than one year, apply directly for a long-term residence permit, not just a long-term visa.

common_mistakes.consequence: Delays in visa processing, potentially missing the start of your studies.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply immediately after receiving your acceptance letter, allowing 60-90 days for processing.
Ensure all documents (except passport) are translated into Czech by a certified translator with an official rubber stamp.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not registering with the Foreign Police on arrival
common_mistakes.consequence: Violation of immigration laws, potential fines or issues with your stay.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Register within 3 days of entry (for visa holders) or 30 days (for D/VR visa holders), unless your accommodation provider handles it.

common_mistakes.consequence: Your permit expires, leading to illegal stay and potential deportation.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Submit the renewal application at least 120 days before the current permit expires, and no later than the last day of its validity.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying for the wrong visa type
common_mistakes.consequence: Application rejection or needing to re-apply for the correct permit.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: If studies last more than one year, apply directly for a long-term residence permit, not a long-term visa.

common_mistakes.consequence: Visa sticker cannot be affixed, or your stay becomes illegal.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure travel medical insurance is valid from the day of entry and maintained throughout your stay.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not having landlord's signature notarized on accommodation proof
common_mistakes.consequence: Document rejection, delaying or rejecting the application.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure the landlord's signature on the proof of accommodation is legalized by a Czech notary or at a Czech Point.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not responding to 'Request to Supplement' in time
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Regularly check email/mail for communication from the Ministry and respond promptly.
